content.problemStatement:
Bitsetbaba Ashiq works in a power grid, which consists of 2K2^K2K power stations (numbered 000 through 2K‚ąí12^K-12K‚ąí1). Initially, there are no connections between the power stations.

One day, Ashiq's boss gave him MMM integers x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xMx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Mx1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xM‚Äč and asked Ashiq to add connections between some power stations. For each two different power stations uuu and vvv, Ashiq should connect them directly if there is a valid index iii such that u‚äēv=xiu \oplus v = x_iu‚äēv=xi‚Äč. Here, ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

To confirm whether Ashiq has completed the task correctly, he should report to his boss the number of connected components in the resulting power grid. Two power stations uuu and vvv are in the same connected component if they are connected directly or indirectly (through other stations).

Ashiq is lazy and he does not want to actually physically perform the task, but he also wants to keep his job. Therefore, he asks for your help. Can you help him find the final number of connected components?
constraints: the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers KKK and MMM.
The second line contains MMM space-separated integers x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xMx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Mx1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xM‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the number of connected components.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are four connected components; each of them contains one of the four nodes.
Example case 2: There are two connections ‚ÄĒ one between nodes 000 and 222 and the other between nodes 111 and 333. Thus, there are two connected components.
